name: Ansible in CI/CD Pipelines
description: Integrate Ansible playbooks into GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or Jenkins pipelines for automated infrastructure deployment on every push.
trendingReason: GitOps and infrastructure-as-code pipelines are becoming the standard for production deployments.

steps.title: Create a requirements file for CI
steps.command:
cat > requirements.yml << 'EOF'
---
collections:
  - name: community.general
  - name: ansible.posix
roles:
  - name: geerlingguy.docker
EOF
steps.description: Pins all role and collection dependencies so CI installs are reproducible.

steps.title: Create a GitHub Actions workflow
steps.command:
mkdir -p .github/workflows && cat > .github/workflows/deploy.yml << 'EOF'
name: Deploy Infrastructure
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths: ["ansible/**"]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install Ansible
        run: pip install ansible
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: ansible-galaxy install -r requirements.yml
      - name: Run playbook
        run: ansible-playbook site.yml -i inventory.ini
        env:
          ANSIBLE_VAULT_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.VAULT_PASSWORD }}
          ANSIBLE_HOST_KEY_CHECKING: "false"
EOF
steps.description: Triggers deployment only when Ansible files change on the main branch.
steps.tip: Store the vault password and SSH private key as GitHub repository secrets.
steps.warning: Never commit vault passwords or SSH keys to version control.

steps.title: Configure SSH key for CI
steps.description: Writes the SSH private key from a CI secret to the runner filesystem.

steps.title: Run a syntax check in CI
steps.description: Validates playbook syntax without executing anything. Catches YAML errors early in the pipeline.

steps.title: Run in check mode as a CI gate
steps.description: Shows what changes would be made without applying them. Useful as a pull request validation step.

steps.title: Lint playbooks with ansible-lint
steps.command: pip install ansible-lint && ansible-lint site.yml roles/
steps.description: Runs ansible-lint to catch best practice violations and potential issues before deployment.

fullScript:
# Ansible CI/CD integration
pip install ansible ansible-lint
ansible-galaxy install -r requirements.yml
ansible-lint site.yml roles/
ansible-playbook site.yml --syntax-check
ansible-playbook site.yml --check --diff
# Full deploy (only in main branch CI)
ansible-playbook site.yml -i inventory.ini

faq.question: How do I pass the vault password in CI without a prompt?
faq.answer: Use a vault password file: 'echo $ANSIBLE_VAULT_PASSWORD > .vault_pass && ansible-playbook site.yml --vault-password-file .vault_pass'. Or set the ANSIBLE_VAULT_PASSWORD_FILE environment variable.

faq.question: Should I run ansible-playbook --check in pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. Running in check mode on PRs shows reviewers exactly what infrastructure changes the code will produce. Combine with --diff for a complete change preview.

faq.question: How do I handle different environments in CI?
faq.answer: Use separate inventory files per environment (inventory/staging.ini, inventory/production.ini) and trigger the appropriate one based on the branch or manual approval gates.
